hero.headline: Stop using spreadsheets. Track your team's attendance in real time.
hero.subheadline: OmniPunt combines secure clock events, approvals, and operational analytics in one workflow.

features.title: Everything your team needs to control attendance
features.subtitle: Mapped to actual app workflows: time entries, approvals, overtime, vacation flow, reports, and admin governance.
features.clockDesc: Capture entries, status changes, and worked-hour summaries with a reliable timeline.
features.faceDesc: Use face-api.js embeddings with guided checks before attendance actions.
features.geoDesc: Validate attendance by GPS radius or terminal QR mode with anti-replay controls.
features.approvalDesc: Approve, reject, or request edits with comments and complete audit history.
features.vacationDesc: Route requests through supervisor and HR stages, with calendar visibility.
features.reportDesc: Generate async CSV exports and monitor key indicators without blocking operations.

how.title: Three simple steps
how.step1Desc: Employees clock in from validated channels with optional facial verification.
how.step2Desc: Supervisors and admins manage pending entries, overtime alerts, and exceptions.
how.step3Desc: Use dashboards and CSV reports to drive payroll and operational decisions.

testimonials.title: Operations teams replaced manual control with confidence
testimonials.quote1: We reduced payroll disputes in the first month because each record has context and approval logs.
testimonials.quote2: Terminal QR and geolocation stopped attendance ambiguity in distributed shifts.
testimonials.quote3: Vacation workflow and overtime alerts made supervisor routines significantly faster.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how OmniPunt collects, uses, stores, and protects personal data while providing attendance and workforce management services.
privacy.section1Body: We process account data (name, email, role), attendance records (clock-in/out timestamps, notes, status), technical metadata (IP and device information), optional geolocation coordinates, vacation and approval workflow records, and facial verification vectors generated through face-api.js. We do not store raw facial video as a persistent attendance record.
privacy.section2Body: Data is used to authenticate users via Supabase Auth, validate attendance events, support supervisor/admin approvals, calculate worked/overtime hours, process vacation requests, generate reports, and maintain system integrity and security monitoring.
privacy.section3Body: Operational records are stored in PostgreSQL and protected by role-based access controls. Authentication is handled by Supabase. We apply access logging, request metadata tracking, and least-privilege controls to reduce unauthorized access risk.
privacy.section4Body: OmniPunt uses browser storage for session continuity, language preferences, and product behavior required for authenticated usage. These technologies are used for platform functionality, not behavioral advertising.
privacy.section5Body: Depending on jurisdiction, users may request access, correction, portability, restriction, or deletion of personal data, subject to legal and operational retention requirements. Requests can be routed through the customer account administrator.
privacy.section6Body: Attendance and audit records are retained according to contractual and legal obligations. Report exports may follow platform retention windows. Data no longer required is deleted or anonymized according to internal procedures.
privacy.section7Body: When infrastructure providers operate across regions, data may be processed in other jurisdictions under contractual safeguards and applicable data protection standards.
privacy.section8Body: For privacy requests or security concerns, contact {{email}}.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern access to and use of OmniPunt by customer organizations and their authorized users.
terms.section1Body: You agree to use the platform only for lawful workforce and attendance management activities. Attempts to bypass security controls, scrape restricted data, or misuse authentication mechanisms are prohibited.
terms.section2Body: Customers are responsible for managing user permissions, safeguarding credentials, and ensuring that submitted attendance and workforce information is accurate and authorized.
terms.section3Body: Paid plans renew according to the selected billing cycle. Customers can cancel renewal at any time before the next billing date. Refunds, when applicable, follow the commercial policy and local mandatory consumer rights.
terms.section4Body: We aim for high availability but cannot guarantee uninterrupted service in all circumstances, including maintenance windows, third-party outages, or force majeure events.
terms.section5Body: Customers remain responsible for compliance with local labor and privacy regulations when configuring attendance, geolocation, and biometric verification policies.
terms.section6Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, OmniPunt and its providers are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including lost profits or business interruption.
terms.section7Body: We may update these Terms periodically. Continued use after an effective update constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
terms.section8Body: For legal or contractual questions, contact {{email}}.

cookies.title: Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.intro: We use cookies and local storage to keep the platform working, measure usage in aggregate form, and improve communication. You can accept, reject, or fine-tune your preferences at any time.

necessary.name: Strictly necessary
necessary.desc: Required for authentication, session continuity, language, and platform security. Cannot be disabled.

analytics.name: Analytics & performance
analytics.desc: Help us understand, in aggregate form, how the platform is used so we can fix issues and prioritize improvements. Loaded only with your consent.

marketing.name: Marketing & communication
marketing.desc: Allow us to measure campaigns, personalize messages, and contact channels. Loaded only with your consent.

consent.title: Data Consent and Processing Policy
consent.intro: This document describes, in plain language, what personal data OmniPunt processes while operating the attendance platform, for what purposes, how long it is retained, with whom it may be shared, and how you can exercise the rights granted by GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) and LGPD (Brazilian Law 13.709/2018).
consent.controllerBody: The data controller is the customer organization that provides OmniPunt to its workforce. OmniPunt acts as a data processor under GDPR and operador under LGPD. To reach our DPO, contact {{email}}.
consent.legalBasisBody: We process data based on: performance of contract (delivering the attendance service), legal and regulatory obligations (workforce time tracking and audit), legitimate interest (security, fraud prevention, and product improvement, always balanced against your rights), and consent (facial biometrics and optional analytics/marketing cookies).
consent.inventoryIntro: The table below lists every category of personal data currently stored by OmniPunt, along with purpose, legal basis, and retention period.

account.fields: ID, name, email, phone, role, status (active/inactive), linked supervisor, organization, creation date
account.purpose: User identification, access control, approval hierarchy, and operational communication.
account.retention: Throughout the contract term + 5 years after termination, in line with labor regulations.

authentication.fields: PIN hash and salt (never stored in plain text), PIN updated-at, failed attempts, temporary lockout, Supabase Auth session data
authentication.purpose: Authenticate users securely and prevent credential misuse.
authentication.legalBasis: Performance of contract and legitimate interest (security)
authentication.retention: While the account is active. Failed attempts are reset on success or once the lockout window expires.

biometric.fields: Mathematical vector (embedding) generated by face-api.js, comparison threshold, last template update
biometric.purpose: Validate the physical presence of the worker at the time of the clock event. We do NOT store photo or video of the face as an attendance record, only the mathematical vector.
biometric.legalBasis: Explicit consent from the data subject (sensitive personal data)
biometric.retention: While the user keeps the biometric enrollment active. Can be deleted at any time without affecting prior records.

photo.fields: Photo file path, last update date
photo.purpose: Visual identification in lists, approvals, and reports.
photo.retention: While the account is active or until the data subject requests deletion.

timeEntries.fields: Clock-in/out timestamps, notes, status (pending/approved/rejected), worked minutes, breaks, overtime (50%/100%), accrued bank hours
timeEntries.purpose: Official workday tracking, overtime calculation, payroll integration, and audit.
timeEntries.legalBasis: Legal obligation (labor regulations) and performance of contract
timeEntries.retention: 5 years after the end of the employment relationship, per applicable labor regulations.

geolocation.fields: Latitude, longitude, and timestamp of the clock event
geolocation.purpose: Validate that the clock event occurred within the radius defined by the account administrator (GPS mode).
geolocation.legalBasis: Performance of contract (when required by the customer) and consent
geolocation.retention: Tied to the matching time entry (5 years). Can be disabled by the account administrator.

ipDevice.fields: Source IP address, device type, operating system, browser, user-agent
ipDevice.purpose: Audit trail, fraud prevention, and security incident investigation.
ipDevice.retention: Full IP: 90 days. After that window, the last octet (IPv4) or last 80 bits (IPv6) are masked, and only the anonymized form is kept for the remaining retention period.

approvalLogs.purpose: Auditable trail of supervisor and admin decisions.
approvalLogs.retention: Same period as the associated time entry (5 years).

bankHours.fields: Entry type, minutes, payment status, paid-at, expiration date, description, author
bankHours.legalBasis: Performance of contract and legal obligation
bankHours.retention: 5 years after the entry or after the balance is paid out/expired.

vacation.fields: Requested period, reason, status, supervisor and HR reviewers, review dates, status change logs
vacation.purpose: Vacation approval workflow and historical record for payroll.
vacation.legalBasis: Performance of contract and legal obligation
vacation.retention: 5 years after the end of the vacation period.

slack.fields: Slack user ID, display name, workspace name
slack.purpose: Deliver operational notifications (clock alerts, approvals, vacation) to the channel configured by the customer.
slack.legalBasis: Performance of contract (configured by the administrator)
slack.retention: While the integration is active. Removed when Slack is disconnected.

billing.fields: Stripe session ID, invoice/subscription ID, payment status, currency, amount, customer email, billing dates
billing.purpose: Process subscriptions, issue invoices, and meet tax requirements.
billing.legalBasis: Performance of contract and legal obligation (tax)
billing.retention: 5 years after the last payment, per applicable tax regulations.

preferences.fields: Selected language, timezone, cookie consent decision
preferences.purpose: Personalize the experience and honor your privacy choices.
preferences.retention: Up to 12 months or until you clear your browser storage.

consent.ipBody: OmniPunt follows data minimization for IP addresses. Different layers apply different policies:
consent.ipBullet1: Analytics cookies: when you allow the analytics category, the IP sent to the measurement service is anonymized at source (anonymize_ip parameter), discarding the last octet before any processing.
consent.ipBullet2: Operational attendance records: the full IP is retained for up to 90 days for audit and incident investigation. After that, the last octet (IPv4) or last 80 bits (IPv6) are masked and only the anonymized form is kept for the remaining retention period.
consent.ipBullet3: Server and security logs: used only for abuse detection, with reduced retention and masking applied on exports.
consent.biometricBody: Facial biometrics are treated as sensitive personal data. They are only collected after explicit consent. We store only the mathematical vector (embedding) generated by face-api.js, not the image or video. The vector cannot be used to reconstruct the original face and is used exclusively to compare a clock event against the enrolled template. You can withdraw consent at any time, which deletes the biometric template without affecting prior attendance records.
consent.retentionBody: Retention follows labor (CLT Art. 7º, XXIX in Brazil; equivalent rules elsewhere), tax, and contractual regulations. In summary: time entries and payroll = 5 years; bank of hours = 5 years after payout; vacation = 5 years after the period ends; billing = 5 years; full IP = 90 days (anonymized afterwards); biometrics = while enrollment is active; photo and preferences = until the data subject requests deletion. After expiry, data is anonymized or deleted following an auditable internal procedure.
consent.processorsBody: To deliver the service, we share strictly necessary data with the following processors:

processor.supabase: Supabase (authentication, PostgreSQL database, and storage) - account and operational data.
processor.stripe: Stripe (payment processing) - admin billing data; OmniPunt does not store card data.
processor.slack: Slack (operational notifications) - only if the integration is enabled by the administrator.
processor.googleFonts: Google Fonts (fonts via CDN) - public interface typography.
processor.analytics: Analytics service (Google Analytics 4 or equivalent) - only if you accept the analytics cookie category.

consent.cookiesBody: We use cookies and localStorage grouped into three categories. Only the necessary category is loaded before your consent. If you do not accept an optional category, no script from that category is loaded or executed.

necessary.name: Strictly necessary
necessary.purpose: Maintain session, language, timezone, and platform security.

analytics.name: Analytics
analytics.purpose: Aggregate usage, failures, and platform performance (loaded only after consent).

marketing.name: Marketing
marketing.purpose: Measure campaigns and acquisition channels (loaded only after consent).
marketing.examples: Pixel and partner cookies, loaded on demand

consent.managePreferencesBody: You can review and change your choices at any time below. Changes apply immediately: scripts from disabled categories are removed from the page.
consent.notDecidedYet: You have not recorded a decision yet.
consent.rightsBody: You can exercise the rights below by contacting your account administrator or directly through {{email}}:

rights.access: Confirm that processing occurs and access your data.
rights.correction: Correct incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated data.
rights.deletion: Request deletion of data processed based on consent, subject to legal retention.
rights.portability: Receive your data in a structured, interoperable format.
rights.objection: Object to processing based on legitimate interest.
rights.withdraw: Withdraw consent (biometrics and optional cookies) at any time, without affecting prior lawful processing.

consent.contactBody: Privacy, security, or rights-related requests should be sent to {{email}}. We respond within 15 business days and, when applicable, route the request to the customer account administrator that acts as the data controller.
